coffees.name: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe Konga
coffees.description: A floral masterpiece from Ethiopia's most celebrated region. The Konga Cooperative produces coffees with unrivalled aromatic complexity — jasmine and bergamot bloom in the cup before giving way to a delicate peach sweetness and a lingering black tea finish.

coffees.name: Colombia Huila El Paraíso
coffees.description: Diego Bermúdez's experimental natural processing transforms Colombia's best cherries into a fruit bomb of extraordinary depth. Strawberry and plum lead before yielding to dark chocolate and a vanilla finish.

coffees.name: Kenya Nyeri Gaturiri
coffees.description: Kenya's volcanic soils and high altitudes produce coffees of striking complexity and power. Gaturiri's washed lots deliver explosive blackcurrant acidity softened by brown sugar sweetness and a cedar-like dry finish.

coffees.name: Panama Gesha La Esmeralda
coffees.description: The coffee that changed the world. La Esmeralda's Gesha variety broke every auction record and redefined what coffee could be. Rose and jasmine aromatics cascade into mandarin brightness, finishing with a long honey sweetness.

coffees.name: Brazil Mantiqueira Yellow Bourbon
coffees.description: Brazil's flagship Yellow Bourbon produces the benchmark for classic sweetness and nutty depth. Low acidity and full body make this the ideal everyday espresso and milk-based drink foundation.

coffees.name: Costa Rica Tarrazú La Pastora
coffees.description: Honey-processed Costa Rica that sits perfectly between the brightness of washed and the fruitiness of natural. Apricot and honey sweetness with a praline finish make this universally loved.

coffees.name: Indonesia Sumatra Mandheling
coffees.description: The quintessential Sumatra: powerful, earthy, and undeniably bold. Wet-hulling gives Mandheling its signature low acidity and extraordinary full body — a coffee that dominates the cup.

coffees.name: Guatemala Antigua La Hermosa
coffees.description: Antigua's volcanic soil and microclimate produce balanced coffees of great depth. Dark chocolate and toffee sweetness with a bright orange peel note — a reliable, satisfying medium roast.

coffees.name: Yemen Mokha Matari
coffees.description: The ancient origin of coffee cultivation. Yemen's Matari coffees are dry-processed on rooftops like they have been for centuries, producing fermented-fruit complexity unlike anything else in the coffee world.

coffees.name: Flash Brew Blend by Tetsu
coffees.description: Designed specifically for flash brew (iced pour-over), this blend is calibrated for brightness and fruit-forward clarity over ice. Citrus and mango sweetness intensify as the brew cools.

coffees.name: Ethiopia Sidama Shantawene
coffees.description: One of the most celebrated natural Ethiopian coffees of recent years. Blueberry and strawberry jam intensity combined with lavender florality and honey sweetness — this cup is a revelation.

coffees.name: Colombia Nariño El Diviso Anaerobic
coffees.description: Jhoan Vergara's anaerobic fermentation experiments have produced one of the most talked-about coffees in recent years. Passion fruit and hibiscus intensity with a rose water finish — polarising, unforgettable.

coffees.name: Rwanda Bourbon Red Hill
coffees.description: Rwanda continues to establish itself as a world-class origin. The Red Hill station's red bourbon lots combine delicate black tea character with hibiscus florality and a clean caramel finish.

coffees.name: Mexico Chiapas San Cristóbal
coffees.description: A dependable and comforting medium roast from the cooperatives of Chiapas. Milk chocolate and pecan with dried cherry sweetness — a coffee that makes mornings better without demanding your full attention.

coffees.name: Burundi Kayanza Kibira
coffees.description: Burundi's terroir shines through in this Kayanza lot. Raspberry and white grape acidity with peach sweetness and a jasmine finish — a coffee that surprises and rewards careful brewing.

coffees.name: Hawaii Kona Peaberry
coffees.description: Hawaii's most famous coffee, grown on the slopes of Mauna Loa. Peaberry beans — single seeds per cherry — concentrate flavour into a smooth, buttery cup with macadamia nut richness and delicate spice.

coffees.name: Tanzania Peaberry Kilimanjaro
coffees.description: Tanzania's classic peaberry profile from the foothills of Kilimanjaro. Dark cherry and plum depth with tangerine brightness and a cedar dry finish. An excellent espresso base.

coffees.name: Peru Cajamarca Tunki
coffees.description: Peru's underrated Cajamarca region produces clean, elegant coffees. Sweet lemon and green grape with white peach sweetness — a perfect introduction to washed South American specialty coffee.

coffees.name: Honduras Copán El Puente
coffees.description: Honduras is Central America's hidden specialty gem. El Puente's honey-processed lots deliver consistent butterscotch sweetness with red apple brightness and an almond finish — approachable and reliable.

coffees.name: Taiwan Alishan High Mountain
coffees.description: Taiwan's mountain coffees are a best-kept secret. Alishan's high altitude and cool temperatures produce delicate cups with osmanthus florality, lychee sweetness, and a uniquely creamy texture.

brewGuides.name: V60 Pour-Over
brewGuides.description: The Hario V60 is the most precise and expressive manual brewing method. Its ridged cone design produces clean, complex cups that showcase a coffee's origin character.

steps.title: Heat & Rinse
steps.description: Heat water to 93°C. Rinse the filter with hot water to remove paper taste and preheat the vessel. Discard rinse water.
steps.tip: Use a gooseneck kettle for precise pouring control.

steps.title: Grind & Dose
steps.description: Grind 20g of coffee to a medium-fine consistency. Level the grounds in the filter.
steps.tip: Freshly ground coffee makes a significant difference. Use beans within 2-4 weeks of roast date.

steps.title: Bloom (0:00-0:45)
steps.description: Pour 40ml of water evenly over all the grounds. This is the bloom — CO2 releases, ensuring even extraction. Wait 45 seconds.
steps.tip: Fresher coffee blooms more vigorously. The dome of foam is a good sign.

steps.title: First Pour (0:45-1:30)
steps.description: Pour in circles from centre outward to 150ml total. Keep the water level consistent.
steps.tip: Pour slowly and steadily. Avoid pouring directly on the filter edges.

steps.title: Second Pour (1:30-2:15)
steps.description: Continue pouring in circles to 300ml total.
steps.tip: The drawdown should finish between 2:45 and 3:15. Adjust your grind if it finishes too fast or slow.

steps.title: Drawdown (2:15-3:00)
steps.description: Allow the brew to fully drain. The bed should be flat and evenly saturated.
steps.tip: Swirl the carafe gently before serving to integrate the brew.

brewGuides.name: Espresso
brewGuides.description: The foundation of café culture. Espresso is a concentrated, pressurised extraction that forms the base of lattes, cappuccinos, and flat whites — or stands alone as the purest coffee experience.

steps.title: Flush & Preheat
steps.description: Run a blank shot through the machine to stabilise temperature. Preheat the portafilter by leaving it in the group head.
steps.tip: Temperature stability is critical. Allow the machine 20-30 minutes to fully heat.

steps.title: Dose & Distribute
steps.description: Dose 18g into the portafilter basket. Use the WDT tool or stir to break up clumps. Level the dose.
steps.tip: Consistent distribution prevents channelling — the main cause of sour or bitter espresso.

steps.title: Tamp
steps.description: Tamp straight and level with 15-20kg of pressure. Ensure the puck surface is perfectly flat.
steps.tip: Consistency matters more than force. Use a calibrated tamper.

steps.title: Extract (0:00-0:30)
steps.description: Lock in the portafilter and start immediately. Target 36ml output in 25-30 seconds.
steps.tip: First drops should appear at 7-10 seconds. If flowing too fast or slow, adjust grind size.

steps.title: Evaluate
steps.description: Taste immediately. Perfect espresso is balanced: sweet, acidic, and slightly bitter in harmony.
steps.tip: Keep a brew journal. Note dose, yield, time, and taste to dial in your recipe.

brewGuides.name: AeroPress
brewGuides.description: The AeroPress is the most versatile manual brewer ever made. Compact, forgiving, and capable of producing espresso-style concentrates to tea-like clarity — perfect for travelers and experimenters.

steps.title: Setup
steps.description: Assemble the AeroPress in the standard position. Insert a filter and rinse with hot water. Place on your mug.
steps.tip: The inverted method (upside down) gives you more control over steep time.

steps.title: Add Coffee
steps.description: Add 17g of medium-ground coffee. Level the grounds.

steps.title: Add Water & Stir
steps.description: Pour 200ml of 85°C water over 30 seconds. Stir 3-4 times.
steps.tip: Lower temperature works well with medium and dark roasts. Use 92°C for lighter roasts.

steps.title: Steep
steps.description: Insert the plunger to stop drip-through. Steep for 60 seconds total (from first pour).

steps.title: Press
steps.description: Press down slowly over 25-30 seconds with steady, even pressure. Stop when you hear a hiss.
steps.tip: Press too fast and the extraction is inconsistent. The hiss means you've pressed through the grounds.

brewGuides.name: French Press
brewGuides.description: The French Press is the most forgiving immersion brewer. No filters, no fuss — just coarse coffee, hot water, and time. Produces a full-bodied, rich cup with natural oils intact.

steps.title: Preheat & Grind
steps.description: Preheat the French Press with hot water. Grind 25g of coffee coarsely.
steps.tip: Coarse grind is essential. Fine grounds lead to over-extraction and bitterness.

steps.title: Add Coffee & Bloom
steps.description: Discard preheat water. Add grounds. Pour just enough water (50ml) to saturate all grounds. Wait 30 seconds.

steps.title: Full Pour & Steep
steps.description: Pour 350ml total water. Place the lid on with the plunger pulled up. Steep 4 minutes total.
steps.tip: Don't press yet — the lid just keeps the heat in.

steps.title: Plunge & Pour
steps.description: Press the plunger down slowly. Pour immediately — don't leave coffee sitting on the grounds.
steps.tip: Leaving coffee sitting after pressing leads to over-extraction. Pour all of it out at once.

brewGuides.name: Chemex
brewGuides.description: The Chemex produces the clearest, cleanest cup of any brewer. Its thick proprietary filter removes all oils and fine particles, revealing the purest expression of a coffee's origin terroir.

steps.title: Fold & Rinse Filter
steps.description: Fold the Chemex filter with 3 layers toward the spout side. Rinse thoroughly with hot water. Discard.
steps.tip: The Chemex filter is much thicker than standard — it needs a thorough rinse to remove paper taste.

steps.title: Dose
steps.description: Add 30g of medium-coarse coffee.

steps.title: Bloom (0:00-0:45)
steps.description: Pour 60ml evenly. Let bloom 45 seconds.
steps.tip: The Chemex shows beautiful bloom patterns through the glass.

steps.title: Pour in Stages (0:45-3:30)
steps.description: Pour in 3 stages of ~140ml each, waiting for drawdown between pours. Target 480ml total.
steps.tip: The Chemex brews slower due to the thick filter. Be patient.

steps.title: Drawdown
steps.description: Allow full drawdown. The total brew should complete between 4-5 minutes.
steps.tip: Serve immediately. The Chemex design keeps it warm, but fresh is always best.

brewGuides.name: Cold Brew
brewGuides.description: Cold brew extracts coffee with cold water over 12-24 hours, producing a naturally sweet, low-acid concentrate. Perfect for hot weather and those sensitive to coffee's acidity.

steps.title: Coarse Grind
steps.description: Grind 100g of coffee very coarsely. The coarser the grind, the cleaner the cold brew.
steps.tip: Medium-dark roasts shine in cold brew — their chocolate notes become intensely sweet and creamy.

steps.title: Combine in Container
steps.description: Add grounds to a mason jar or cold brew vessel. Pour 700ml of cold filtered water. Stir gently.

steps.title: Steep in Fridge (12-24 hours)
steps.description: Cover and refrigerate. Steep 16-18 hours for optimal balance — adjust to taste.
steps.tip: Longer steep = stronger. If it tastes too strong, steep less or dilute with water when serving.

steps.title: Filter
steps.description: Filter through a fine mesh strainer, then through paper or cloth filter. Pour into serving vessel.

steps.title: Serve
steps.description: Serve over ice straight, or dilute 1:1 with water or milk. Keeps for 2 weeks refrigerated.
steps.tip: Cold brew concentrate is very forgiving — experiment with ratios to find your perfect strength.
